questions.question: Write down all the factors of 24.
questions.solution: Factors are numbers that divide 24 exactly: $24 = 1 \times 24$, $2 \times 12$, $3 \times 8$, $4 \times 6$.

questions.question: List the first five multiples of 7.
questions.solution: Multiples are $7 \times 1, 7 \times 2, 7 \times 3, 7 \times 4, 7 \times 5$.

questions.question: Which number is neither prime nor composite?
questions.solution: A prime number must have exactly two factors and a composite number more than two. Since 1 has only one factor (itself), it fits neither category.

questions.question: Find all prime numbers between 40 and 50.
questions.solution: Checking each number: 41, 43, and 47 have no factors other than 1 and themselves. 42 (even), 44 (even), 45 (multiple of 5), 46 (even), 48 (even), and 49 ($7 \times 7$) are composite.

questions.question: Are 15 and 28 co-prime? Give reasons.
questions.solution: Factors of 15 are 1, 3, 5, 15. Factors of 28 are 1, 2, 4, 7, 14, 28. The only common factor is 1, so they are co-prime.

questions.question: Is 28 a perfect number? Prove it by finding the sum of its factors.
questions.solution: Factors of 28 are 1, 2, 4, 7, 14, and 28. Sum = $1 + 2 + 4 + 7 + 14 + 28 = 56$. Since $56 = 2 \times 28$, it is a perfect number.

questions.question: I am a number less than 40. One of my factors is 7. The sum of my digits is 8. Who am I?
questions.solution: Multiples of 7 less than 40 are 7, 14, 21, 28, 35. Sum of digits: $7 \to 7$, $1+4 \to 5$, $2+1 \to 3$, $2+8 \to 10$, $3+5 \to 8$. The number is 35.

questions.question: Give the prime factorisation of 56.
questions.solution: $56 = 2 \times 28 = 2 \times 2 \times 14 = 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 7$.

questions.question: Using prime factorisation, determine if 168 is divisible by 12.
questions.solution: Prime factorisation of 168: $2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 7$. Prime factorisation of 12: $2 \times 2 \times 3$. Since all prime factors of 12 (two 2s and one 3) are present in the factorisation of 168, it is divisible.

questions.question: Which of these numbers is the product of exactly three distinct prime numbers: 45, 60, 91, 105?
questions.solution: $45 = 3 \times 3 \times 5$ (not distinct), $60 = 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 5$ (four factors), $91 = 7 \times 13$ (two factors), $105 = 3 \times 5 \times 7$ (three distinct prime factors).

questions.question: What is the first common multiple of 4 and 6?
questions.solution: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... The first common multiple is 12.

questions.question: Test the divisibility of 8560 by 8.
questions.solution: Check the last three digits: 560. Since $560 \div 8 = 70$, the original number is divisible by 8.

questions.question: Find two numbers whose product is 10,000 such that neither number has 0 as its units digit.
Name three pairs of twin primes between 1 and 50.
questions.solution: Twin primes are primes with a difference of 2.

questions.question: Why is the year 2100 not a leap year?
questions.answer: It is divisible by 100 but not by 400.
questions.solution: The rule for leap years is: multiples of 4 are leap years, except for centurial years which must also be multiples of 400.

questions.question: Find the smallest number that is a multiple of all numbers from 1 to 10 except for 7.
questions.solution: We need the L.C.M. of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10. Excluding 7, the primes involved are 2, 3, 5. Max powers: $2^3$, $3^2$, $5^1$. Product = $8 \times 9 \times 5 = 360$.

questions.question: Find the smallest number that is a multiple of all numbers from 1 to 10.
questions.solution: This is L.C.M(1, 10), which includes the factor 7. So, $360 \times 7 = 2520$.

questions.question: In thread art with 12 pegs, if you tie the thread to every 4th peg, will you eventually touch every peg?
questions.solution: If the number of pegs (12) and the gap (4) have common factors other than 1, you will only visit a subset of pegs ($12 \div 4 = 3$, so only 3 pegs will be touched).

questions.question: Find seven consecutive composite numbers between 1 and 100.
questions.solution: This is the longest gap between primes below 100, occurring after 89.

questions.question: Is the statement 'All even numbers are composite' true or false? Explain.
questions.solution: The number 2 is an even number, but it is prime because its only factors are 1 and 2.
